Okay, welcome back. So in this lesson, I wanted to walk you through the high level map, everything that is happening within this coaching, the conversion, enrollment, and essentially what it's designed to do. And the rest of the program is really going to be dedicated to going deeper into all these elements and really equipping you with the tools, the skills, the techniques for effectively leading it, leading that element of the transformation. So without further ado, the CTC Enrollment Framework really consists of five parts. The first part is really the pre-call, where we go into greater detail in application funnel amplifiers. Part two is really the six milestones that essentially have to be achieved from the moment the call starts, right, until the end, right? If we're going to get the empowered close, there are six milestones that we want our prospect to achieve and walk through and experience. And as we discussed, these aren't linear, right? These are milestones that can be equated to the kind of like these tipping point moments, these ahas, these epiphanies that might happen within 20 seconds, or they might take a little bit longer for someone to become receptive and open to them. But we can, of course, shorten the amount of time it takes and create the conditions in which these milestones can be achieved. And these are what I call the metastates. These are the states that we're creating right off the bat, and we're facilitating and maintaining throughout the call. So we're going to go deeper into what those are. And then the permissions, the three permissions. Everything is permission-based or invitation-based. And that is a way that we facilitate and create and maintain safety throughout the call, which is one of the metastates. So you're going to see what that is all about. And then finally, post-call. This is the domain of the Tyler Durden follow-up. So this is essentially what's going to happen after the call. And of course, we go deeper in that in automated intimacy as well. So this is what it looks like on a high-level map. I know there's a lot going on here, so we're going to break down these pieces real quick. We got that timeline on the horizontal access point A and point B. This is essentially the transformational journey that we're leading our prospect on, and all the things that have to happen in between. We have the application funnel amplifiers happening before the call. And this is going to be designed to coach mastery, right? That you do have full grasp on this topic, and you do have the ability to help solve that problem or help guide them towards that outcome. That mastery should have been coached, obviously, on anything they've consumed before getting on a call with you, as well as what is happening in the lead-up to that call. Magnetism. It's where they make the decision, right? Where if it's going to be anybody, it's going to be you. They feel magnetic to you, your content, your grasp of the topic, your mastery, your ability to see them, witness them, care for them. All that comes online with magnetism that can be coached pre-call. And then momentum. If they have achieved a degree of momentum towards a point of no return where backtracking or staying in that old reality just makes no sense, right? And has more friction than moving forward into your program and working with you, that is also something that we want to coach in a pre-call scenario. So that is happening pre-call, where we're coaching mastery, magnetism, and momentum. Once the call starts, there's really two core parts on the call. Each of them can be anywhere between 10 to 30 minutes. That, of course, doesn't mean that we need a decision by the end of the call. That's where the Tyler Durden follow-up comes into play, right? Where we could be relaxed in letting someone off the call if part of their decision-making process they need a day or two to reflect. They need to have their necessary conversations. They need to depressurize. We have the Tyler Durden follow-up in place to help reinforce that decision-making accountability. But on the call itself, there are six milestones that we will be coaching from the beginning of the call, till the end, those are resonance, relevance, and resourcefulness. These are all milestones someone has to achieve to give an empowered yes. And those first three are going to be essentially unlocked and coached in part one of the call, which we call unpacking the situational distalt. This is where we take the totality of all the things that are represented by the outcome or the problem they have, right? Where the problem and the outcome or the problem and the solution, the desire and the pain, these are inseparable. They're all part of a gestalt which we're going to go deeper into, but this is the technique we're going to be using to coach and create resonance, relevance, and resourcefulness, whereas part two is going to be leading your prospect to actualize all that potential. Because once that situational gestalt is unpacked and we have moved someone more towards desire anticipation, renewed possibility, renewed confidence, clarity, readiness, and rightness, all of a sudden that is a lot of active, latent potential. Part two is helping them actualize it by sharing the integrated offer. The offer that has integrated everything in part one has taken it into account where we could present them investment options, co-create an opportunity and an investment option that works for them, address any concerns. And then of course, hold them and ourselves accountable to that 100% close, setting appropriate boundaries that a decision ought to be made for their own benefit, whether that's a yes or a no or maybe later and what's happening in the meantime. So those are the two core parts of the call. In that second part, we are coaching achievability, accountability, and ultimately actualization. Each one, as I said, could be anywhere between 10 minutes each, could be longer, and we're not forcing decisions or making it work at a rigid pace that is a natural to actually have the conversation is flowing. And within all of this, there are these meta states that facilitate it, right? We need someone to be connected to a sense of possibility if any of this is going to work. This is really the driving force of the call is they believe there is a possibility that can be available to them if they had the right guidance, if they had the right opportunity. So this is something we are establishing early. It is what is driving us forward on this call. A sense of safety is a meta state that is necessary. No one can dive into the dark chasms of their old experience and their doubts and their fears and have those transmuted into readiness and clarity, unless they feel safe putting those on the table, unless they feel safe with you as their coach, unless they feel safe within themselves in showing up to this call. So that is a state we will be establishing and maintaining. And then of course, power, right? People who are making powerful decisions, powerful life changing decisions need to feel powerful. And this is where the whole notion of disempowering our prospects doesn't serve us. You can't strip away someone's power and the maximum to make powerful decisions. You can only force them into complying with what you are actually trying to force them to do. And that is no longer at our disposal. So we are going to be establishing and helping them maintain and connect to their sense of power. These are the three meta states and then three permissions throughout, right? Permission right at the beginning of the call. Once we talk about the agenda, right? Is this cool? Can we move forward? Does this feel good? Any concerns about this permission to about? Can we talk about this offer? Do you feel safe? Do you feel ready to explore this opportunity together and then permission three to move forward? Right? What would actually feel good right now? Does it feel good to explore the investment options? So these are all the things going on within the CTC enrollment framework. And in the rest of the program, we're going to go deeper in breaking down these main components. So next up, we're going to go deeper into each of those meta states of coaching and facilitating a state of safety of power and a possibility in module three. That's going to be the medius one here where we're going to go into this terrain of unpacking the situational gestalt. You're not going to need a PhD in psychology to understand this. In fact, as we go through it, you're probably going to see that this actually is naturally what is occurring when people talk about a problem or an outcome, how these are just kind of surface level placeholders for a much deeper, richer, intertwined experience that is represented by the entirety of a situational gestalt. So we'll go deeper into that along with all sorts of coaching techniques to really unpack it and shift people more to that right column here from longing to desire, from anxiety to anticipation, from doubt to renewed possibility, failure to renewed confidence, confusion to clarity, resistance to readiness and wrongness to rightness. So it will be well equipped to guide that transformation. And in part two, actualizing potential, we're going to discuss it in module four, essentially how to coach people towards that decisiveness, towards being accountable, towards the actualization of all this potential that is now existent and available to them. So that is the ride we will be taking between now and the rest of the program. Let this sit, let this digest. I know we put a lot of new paradigms and ideas on the table and now we're going to go deeper into each one of them. So thanks for being along on this journey and I'll catch you in the next video. Bye for now.
